title: Errors 4
description: The `map_err` method lets you transform one error type into another, which is useful when combining different error types.
instructions: Use map_err to convert the error type so both operations return the same error type.
hints: Check if the value is negative first, then if it's zero.
Return the appropriate CreationError variant for each case.
Cast with `as u64` for the success case since the value is confirmed positive.
explanation: Custom error types help describe what went wrong in domain-specific terms. Using an enum for errors lets you represent multiple failure modes with a single type. The `map_err` method on Result can convert between error types: `result.map_err(|e| MyError::from(e))`. This is essential when combining operations that produce different error types.
